form.instruction: Fill the 9-section campaign brief, or let AI auto-generate.

messageMatrix.label: Message Matrix (optional)
messageMatrix.help: Upload a .xlsx file to auto-fill project name/context and leverage USP info during analysis.

error.nameAndContextRequired: Please enter the Project Name and Project Context first.
error.projectNameShort: Project name must be at least 3 meaningful characters.
error.contextShort: Project context must be at least 20 characters and 5 words.
error.autogenFailed: AI auto-generation failed. Please try again later.

context.title: 1. Project Context
context.guide:
Explain why this project is needed now — its background and importance.

Focus on any of the following that apply:
- New product launch: which product/service is being introduced?
- Consumer insight: what trend or shift in needs was observed recently?
- Competitive response: what competitor move does this campaign counter?
- Season/event: is it tied to a specific season or sponsorship?
- Brand strategy: any strategic intent such as repositioning or awareness expansion?

Tip: Answer "Why are we running this campaign?" succinctly, as if briefing an executive.

objective.title: 2. Objective
objective.guide:
State the campaign objectives across three layers. Each objective must be measurable.

[Commercial] Business targets such as revenue, share, repeat purchase
[Behavior] Actions you want viewers of the ad to take
[Attitudinal] Perception changes toward the brand or product

audience.title: 3. Audience
audience.guide: Define the audience concretely — demographics (age/gender/income/region), lifestyle, purchase behavior, pain points. For multiple targets, label Primary / Secondary priority.

keyMessage.title: 4. Key Message
keyMessage.guide: The single most important message to deliver — 1-2 sentences. Express it as a benefit (not a feature). Answer: "If they remember only one thing, what is it?"

proofPoints.title: 5. Proof Points
proofPoints.guide: 1-3 supporting evidence items for the Key Message. Choose from technical advantages, awards/certifications, results, testimonials, or expert endorsements. Include numbers and sources when possible.

mandatories.title: 6. Mandatories
mandatories.guide: Mandatory media/channel requirements, asset specs, brand guidelines, legal / regulatory constraints, and similar items.

budget.title: 7. Budget
budget.guide: Total budget and allocation by media channel. Include year-over-year delta if useful.

marketNeeds.title: 8. Market Needs
marketNeeds.guide: Markets where the ad will run and localization requirements — target countries/languages, cultural considerations, local competitive landscape.

timing.title: 9. Timing
timing.guide: Campaign run dates, key milestones (product launch, CES/IFA, etc.), and phase plan (Teasing/Launch/Sustain).

projectName.label: Project Name
projectName.placeholder: e.g., LG OLED TV Global Launch

objectiveCommercial.label: Commercial
objectiveCommercial.placeholder: e.g., North America OLED revenue +15% YoY, 45% premium share

objectiveBehavior.label: Behavior
objectiveBehavior.placeholder: e.g., +20% product page visits, 10,000 in-store demo reservations

keyMessage.placeholder: The single message consumers must remember

marketNeeds.placeholder: e.g., US/Germany/India — 3 markets, per-market adaptation required
